description_long: This rustic cabin features a steeply pitched, thatched gable roof with visible wooden support beams and a weathered texture. The front facade displays a small wooden door, flanked by rough-hewn logs and partially sheltered by the roof's deep overhang. The side views reveal the thick, textured thatch covering the entire roof, with wooden beams protruding horizontally at regular intervals. The structure is primarily constructed from natural wood and thatch, giving it an earthy, traditional appearance. There are no visible windows, and the cabin's compact form suggests a simple, utilitarian interior. The overall design evokes a primitive, hand-built shelter suitable for rural or historical settings.
description: A rustic, hand-built wooden cabin with a steep thatched gable roof, deep overhangs, and a small wooden door on the front facade. The structure is compact, with prominent wooden beams and no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the steep gable roof, central wooden door, and exposed log framework beneath the thatch.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the thick, textured thatch roof and protruding horizontal wooden beams; no doors or windows visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the gable roof and log structure visible but no door or windows present.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thatched roof and wooden beams without any openings.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with thick thatched covering and deep overhangs, supported by exposed wooden beams
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin; the structure appears windowless from all views shown
attribute_door_placement: A single wooden door is centered on the front facade beneath the gable roof overhang
